description: C++17 standard is enforced (CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17, REQUIRED ON, EXTENSIONS OFF)
CMakePresets.json defines configure presets for debug, release, and test
CMakePresets.json defines build presets inheriting from each configure preset
CMakePresets.json defines a test preset that runs CTest with --output-on-failure
Test configure preset enables coverage flags (--coverage or -fprofile-arcs -ftest-coverage)
GTest v1.14+ is available via FetchContent under the test configuration
All three presets configure and build with zero warnings under -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic
cmake --preset test && ctest --preset test executes successfully
Application launches and displays a window titled "Calculator"
Window dimensions are 320x480 pixels
Window is not resizable
Closing the window exits the application with code 0
Application class is separate from Calculator class (no calculator logic in Application)
Calculator class stub exists with default constructor and destructor
No memory leaks or resource leaks on shutdown
Builds cleanly with all three CMake presets from ticket 1
Display shows "0" on initial state and after clear
Pressing digit buttons updates the display with the entered number
Leading zeros are suppressed (except "0.")
Decimal point appends correctly and cannot be entered twice per number
All four arithmetic operations (+, -, x, /) produce correct results
Operator chaining evaluates intermediate results before applying the new operator
Division by zero displays "Error" and blocks input until Clear
Clear resets display to "0" and restores full functionality from any state
Keyboard keys 0-9, ., +, -, *, /, Enter/=, Escape/C map to calculator actions
Calculator class has no dependency on any GUI headers or libraries
Display shows up to 10 significant digits with trailing zeros stripped
tests/CalculatorTest.cpp exists and compiles under the test preset
All digits 0-9 are individually tested for entry and display
All four arithmetic operations are tested with at least 2 cases each
Division by zero produces "Error" and blocks further input until Clear
Operator chaining is tested with at least 2 chain scenarios
Clear resets from every state (mid-entry, post-result, post-error)
Decimal point behavior is tested (entry, leading decimal, duplicate rejection)
Coverage report can be generated via the test preset toolchain
Calculator.cpp achieves 100% line coverage
Calculator.h achieves 100% line coverage (all inline methods exercised)
All tests pass when run via ctest --preset test
.github/workflows/ci.yml exists and is valid YAML
CI triggers on push to main and pull requests targeting main
All three presets build successfully in CI with zero warnings (-Werror)
Test results are reported in the CI job summary (JUnit XML via --gtest_output)
Coverage report is generated and uploaded as a build artifact
Pipeline fails if Calculator.cpp line coverage drops below 100%
Traceability summary artifact is generated with commit SHA, test counts, and coverage data
